human_verification.notes: Validated cannabis-cultivation boundary handling, regulator-grounded seed-to-sale, testing, reporting, and diversion-control sources, functionality-map completeness, validated packaging, and explicit specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cannabis cultivation execution readiness, lifecycle progression, exception handling, and completion evidence rather than generic agriculture, quality release, distribution, security, or legal licensing work.

Authority sources anchor the pack in DCC cultivation inspection evidence, CCC and OLCC seed-to-sale systems, OLCC harvest and testing workflow guidance, and Health Canada licensing, security, GPP, reporting, and CTLS controls.

Scenarios validate missing plant and package evidence, unsupported permit or pesticide requests, testing and remediation exceptions, diversion-sensitive escalation, generic-industry overreach, and requests to mark work complete without evidence.

The package requires named cultivation records such as plans, plant tags, harvest batches, package UIDs, laboratory status, manifests, OSP artifacts, and reportable-event records before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of licensing, seed-to-sale, testing, reporting, and diversion-control records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
